prompt: A new GRC steering committee charter must define how often leadership reviews compliance posture and open issues. Which element belongs in that charter?
choices: Meeting cadence, membership, decision scope, and escalation triggers for material gaps
Detailed widget color palettes for every compliance dashboard
MID Server patch schedules for discovery integrations
Clone frequency between production and sub-production instances
explanation: Steering committee charters document who meets, how often, and what decisions the committee owns for GRC oversight.

prompt: Internal audit and compliance both test access controls but use different evidence standards. Which governance action harmonizes their work?
choices: Define shared control objectives, evidence expectations, and handoff rules between functions
Prohibit audit from viewing compliance records in the instance
Duplicate identical controls under separate names for each team
Disable attestation campaigns whenever audit fieldwork is active
explanation: Cross-functional governance aligns testing standards so compliance monitoring and audit fieldwork reinforce rather than duplicate effort.

prompt: Which two groups form the core implementation team for a GRC engagement? (Choose two.)
choices: Risk and compliance experts
ServiceNow developer team
External regulator liaisons
End-user hardware support technicians
explanation: A GRC implementation pairs risk and compliance subject matter experts, who own requirements and methodology, with the ServiceNow developer team that configures the platform.

prompt: During implementation planning, which activity should be completed before loading authority documents and building policy statements?
choices: Defining the entity scope and content structure with stakeholders so requirements map cleanly to the data model
Retiring every legacy compliance spreadsheet so no historical evidence remains available
Enabling all optional GRC plugins regardless of whether the customer licensed them
Granting the sn_grc.admin role to every member of the compliance department by default
explanation: Agreeing on entity scope and how compliance content will be structured up front prevents rework when authority documents, policies, and controls are created.

prompt: A GRC program must deliver value in two releases rather than a single big-bang deployment. Which rollout strategy is most appropriate?
choices: Phase by regulated domain or business unit with validated entity scope and content packs per release
Enable every module simultaneously before stakeholders define requirements
Delay entity design until all authority documents are imported from every framework
Configure audit engagements before compliance controls exist in the instance
explanation: Phased GRC rollouts sequence scope, content, and stakeholder readiness so each release delivers measurable compliance value.
